data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to include the generated file in compilation via `<Compile>`:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />`.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>

    </ItemGroup>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target so the generated file is included in the compilation. The key addition in the `.csproj`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This must be inside the target (not at the project level) because the property `_GeneratedFilePath` is only defined within the target, and `Compile` items added inside a `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` target are picked up by the compiler.
